content: agent1: implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py (StreamListener class: __init__, receive(), new tick() method). Will add is_last_chunk=True on timeout chunk IF that field already exists on StreamResponse (feature1), otherwise skip it - let me know if/when you add is_last_chunk to StreamResponse in messages.py so I can wire it in tick(). No on_chunk callback found in codebase currently.

agent1: my diff to dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py: (1) added 'import time' at top; (2) StreamListener.__init__ gets two new params idle_timeout_s: float | None = None, and sets self.idle_timeout_s + self._last_chunk_ts = None at the end of __init__; (3) in receive(), right after the try/except that extracts chunk_message (before the start_identifier check), added 'if chunk_message: self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()'; (4) added a new method tick(self, now_ts=None) between receive() and flush() that emits StreamResponse(...,chunk='') and unconditionally resets stream_start/stream_end/cache_hit/field_start_queue/field_end_queue/_last_chunk_ts when idle_timeout_s elapses. No is_last_chunk field involved (doesn't exist yet). Since you're adding debug logging throughout the same methods, please rebase/merge carefully around __init__ and receive() - happy to coordinate order if needed.
